title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · Data Contract Brief: Set Operations
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql set operations increment in a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics.
Define anchor, recursive step, termination, and cycle or depth safeguards for hierarchical queries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 4 rows, and a reconciliation total. Competency probe: Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics. A defensible solution applies Set Operations, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-set-operations — Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics.
WITH team_counts AS (
  SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS ticket_count
  FROM tickets
  GROUP BY team_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COALESCE(team_counts.ticket_count, 0) AS ticket_count
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN team_counts USING (team_id)
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM tickets
  WHERE tickets.team_id = teams.team_id AND status = 'investigating'
)
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

ORDER BY ticket_id;
